title: Executive Order 8457—Authorizing Certain Employees of the General Accounting Office To Acquire a Classified Civil-Service Status

flags.justification: Exercises a specific statutory authority for a defined category of already-serving employees, bounded by four explicit qualifying conditions.
Requires recommendation by the Comptroller General plus four explicit qualifying conditions (continuous satisfactory service, noncompetitive test, citizenship, non-disqualification).
Single purpose applied to a defined employee category.
This defines eligibility criteria for a status conversion, not an enforcement regime against violators.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A one-time conversion to permanent civil-service status, not an ongoing temporary-authority grant.

The order explicitly requires that future vacancies 'be filled only in accordance with the competitive civil-service requirements' — strengthening rather than weakening merit-system protections going forward.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A bounded, one-time personnel-regularization action typical of the era, converting emergency and NIRA-era appointees to permanent status as New Deal agencies were regularized.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
This is bounded to already-serving employees meeting specific criteria and explicitly does not create an ongoing exemption pathway for future hires.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/8 = 0.0%. A well-designed batch personnel-regularization order that explicitly strengthens future merit-system requirements even as it regularizes past emergency-era appointments.
